title: Fixing a Route Parameter Middleware Issue in a Complex Express API

statement.markdown: You are given an Express API with multiple routes, some of which use route parameters. However, the routes are not properly ordered, causing the route parameter middleware to be applied unnecessarily. Your task is to fix the order of the routes so that the route parameter middleware is only applied when necessary.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a series of URLs, each representing a route in the Express API.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be the corrected route order as an array of strings.
statement.notes: The corrected route order should ensure that the route parameter middleware is only applied when the route parameters are present in the URL.

constraints.description: The number of routes in the Express API

examples.explanation: Route parameter middleware should be applied
Route parameter middleware should be applied
Route parameter middleware should not be applied

hints.text: Check the order of the routes in the Express API
Verify that the route parameter middleware is only applied when necessary

editorial.title: Fixing the Route Parameter Middleware Issue
editorial.intuition: The key to fixing the route parameter middleware issue is to ensure that the routes are ordered correctly. This can be achieved by placing the routes with route parameters after the routes without route parameters.
editorial.bruteForce: One approach to fixing the issue is to manually reorder the routes based on the presence of route parameters.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution is to use a regular expression to match the routes and apply the route parameter middleware only when necessary.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a middleware function that checks for the presence of route parameters and applies the middleware only when necessary.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proven by checking that the route parameter middleware is only applied when the route parameters are present in the URL.
editorial.dryRun: A dry run of the solution can be performed by testing the corrected route order with a series of URLs.
commonMistakes: Not checking the order of the routes
Not applying the route parameter middleware correctly
